lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਿਨੑੀ ਕੰਮੀ ਨਾਹਿ ਗੁਣ ਤੇ ਕੰਮੜੇ ਵਿਸਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jinaee kamee naeh gun te kamarre visaar |
lines.english: Fareed, those deeds which do not bring merit - forget about those deeds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਈ ਜਾਇ ਸਮੑਾਲਿ ਜਿਥੈ ਹੀ ਤਉ ਵੰਞਣਾ ॥੫੮॥
lines.translit: saaee jaae samaal jithai hee tau vanyanaa |58|
lines.english: Remember that place where you must go. ||58||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਤੁ ਸਰਮਿੰਦਾ ਥੀਵਹੀ ਸਾਂਈ ਦੈ ਦਰਬਾਰਿ ॥੫੯॥
lines.translit: mat saramindaa theevahee saanee dai darabaar |59|
lines.english: Otherwise, you shall be put to shame, in the Court of the Lord. ||59||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਦੀ ਕਰਿ ਚਾਕਰੀ ਦਿਲ ਦੀ ਲਾਹਿ ਭਰਾਂਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa saahib dee kar chaakaree dil dee laeh bharaand |
lines.english: Fareed, work for your Lord and Master; dispel the doubts of your heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਾਲੇ ਮੈਡੇ ਕਪੜੇ ਕਾਲਾ ਮੈਡਾ ਵੇਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kaale maidde kaparre kaalaa maiddaa ves |
lines.english: Fareed, my clothes are black, and my outfit is black.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਵੇਸਾਂ ਨੋ ਲੋੜੀਐ ਰੁਖਾਂ ਦੀ ਜੀਰਾਂਦਿ ॥੬੦॥
lines.translit: daravesaan no lorreeai rukhaan dee jeeraand |60|
lines.english: The dervishes, the humble devotees, have the patient endurance of trees. ||60||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਤੀ ਤੋਇ ਨ ਪਲਵੈ ਜੇ ਜਲਿ ਟੁਬੀ ਦੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: tatee toe na palavai je jal ttubee dee |
lines.english: The crop which is burnt will not bloom, even if it is soaked in water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਨਹੀ ਭਰਿਆ ਮੈ ਫਿਰਾ ਲੋਕੁ ਕਹੈ ਦਰਵੇਸੁ ॥੬੧॥
lines.translit: gunahee bhariaa mai firaa lok kahai daraves |61|
lines.english: I wander around full of sins, and yet people call me a dervish - a holy man. ||61||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਕੁਆਰੀ ਤਾ ਚਾਉ ਵੀਵਾਹੀ ਤਾਂ ਮਾਮਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: jaan kuaaree taa chaau veevaahee taan maamale |
lines.english: When she is a virgin, she is full of desire; but when she is married, then her troubles begin.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੋ ਡੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਰਬ ਦੀ ਝੂਰੇਦੀ ਝੂਰੇਇ ॥੬੨॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jo ddohaagan rab dee jhooredee jhooree |62|
lines.english: Fareed, she who is forsaken by her Husband Lord, grieves and laments. ||62||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਏਹੋ ਪਛੋਤਾਉ ਵਤਿ ਕੁਆਰੀ ਨ ਥੀਐ ॥੬੩॥
lines.translit: fareedaa eho pachhotaau vat kuaaree na theeai |63|
lines.english: Fareed, she has this one regret, that she cannot be a virgin again. ||63||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਿੰਜੂ ਬੋੜਨੑਿ ਨਾ ਪੀਵਹਿ ਉਡਣ ਸੰਦੀ ਡੰਝ ॥੬੪॥
lines.translit: chinjoo borrana naa peeveh uddan sandee ddanjh |64|
lines.english: They dip in their bills, but do not drink; they fly away, still thirsty. ||64||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਲਰ ਕੇਰੀ ਛਪੜੀ ਆਇ ਉਲਥੇ ਹੰਝ ॥
lines.translit: kalar keree chhaparree aae ulathe hanjh |
lines.english: The swans have landed in a small pond of salt water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੰਸੁ ਉਡਰਿ ਕੋਧ੍ਰੈ ਪਇਆ ਲੋਕੁ ਵਿਡਾਰਣਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: hans uddar kodhrai peaa lok viddaaran jaae |
lines.english: The swans fly away, and land in the fields of grain. The people go to chase them away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਹਿਲਾ ਲੋਕੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਦਾ ਹੰਸੁ ਨ ਕੋਧ੍ਰਾ ਖਾਇ ॥੬੫॥
lines.translit: gahilaa lok na jaanadaa hans na kodhraa khaae |65|
lines.english: The thoughtless people do not know, that the swans do not eat the grain. ||65||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਸਰੁ ਭਰਿਆ ਭੀ ਚਲਸੀ ਥਕੇ ਕਵਲ ਇਕਲ ॥੬੬॥
lines.translit: fareedaa sar bhariaa bhee chalasee thake kaval ikal |66|
lines.english: Fareed, the overflowing pool shall also pass away, and only the lotus flowers shall remain. ||66||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਲਿ ਚਲਿ ਗਈਆਂ ਪੰਖੀਆਂ ਜਿਨੑੀ ਵਸਾਏ ਤਲ ॥
lines.translit: chal chal geean pankheean jinaee vasaae tal |
lines.english: The birds which lived in the pools have flown away and left.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਇਟ ਸਿਰਾਣੇ ਭੁਇ ਸਵਣੁ ਕੀੜਾ ਲੜਿਓ ਮਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa itt siraane bhue savan keerraa larrio maas |
lines.english: Fareed, a stone will be your pillow, and the earth will be your bed. The worms shall eat into your flesh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਜੁਗ ਵਾਪਰੇ ਇਕਤੁ ਪਇਆ ਪਾਸਿ ॥੬੭॥
lines.translit: ketarriaa jug vaapare ikat peaa paas |67|
lines.english: Countless ages will pass, and you will still be lying on one side. ||67||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਭੰਨੀ ਘੜੀ ਸਵੰਨਵੀ ਟੁਟੀ ਨਾਗਰ ਲਜੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa bhanee gharree savanavee ttuttee naagar laj |
lines.english: Fareed, your beautiful body shall break apart, and the subtle thread of the breath shall be snapped.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜਰਾਈਲੁ ਫਰੇਸਤਾ ਕੈ ਘਰਿ ਨਾਠੀ ਅਜੁ ॥੬੮॥
lines.translit: ajaraaeel faresataa kai ghar naatthee aj |68|
lines.english: In which house will the Messenger of Death be a guest today? ||68||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਭੰਨੀ ਘੜੀ ਸਵੰਨਵੀ ਟੂਟੀ ਨਾਗਰ ਲਜੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa bhanee gharree savanavee ttoottee naagar laj |
lines.english: Fareed, your beautiful body shall break apart, and the subtle thread of the breath shall be snapped.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਸਜਣ ਭੁਇ ਭਾਰੁ ਥੇ ਸੇ ਕਿਉ ਆਵਹਿ ਅਜੁ ॥੬੯॥
lines.translit: jo sajan bhue bhaar the se kiau aaveh aj |69|
lines.english: Those friends who were a burden on the earth - how can they come today? ||69||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬਹੀ ਚਲਿ ਨ ਆਇਆ ਪੰਜੇ ਵਖਤ ਮਸੀਤਿ ॥੭੦॥
lines.translit: kabahee chal na aaeaa panje vakhat maseet |70|
lines.english: You never come to the mosque for your five daily prayers. ||70||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਬੇ ਨਿਵਾਜਾ ਕੁਤਿਆ ਏਹ ਨ ਭਲੀ ਰੀਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa be nivaajaa kutiaa eeh na bhalee reet |
lines.english: Fareed: O faithless dog, this is not a good way of life.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਸਿਰੁ ਸਾਂਈ ਨਾ ਨਿਵੈ ਸੋ ਸਿਰੁ ਕਪਿ ਉਤਾਰਿ ॥੭੧॥
lines.translit: jo sir saanee naa nivai so sir kap utaar |71|
lines.english: The head which does not bow to the Lord - chop off and remove that head. ||71||

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਠੁ ਫਰੀਦਾ ਉਜੂ ਸਾਜਿ ਸੁਬਹ ਨਿਵਾਜ ਗੁਜਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: autth fareedaa ujoo saaj subah nivaaj gujaar |
lines.english: Rise up, Fareed, and cleanse yourself; chant your morning prayer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਸਿਰੁ ਸਾਈ ਨਾ ਨਿਵੈ ਸੋ ਸਿਰੁ ਕੀਜੈ ਕਾਂਇ ॥
lines.translit: jo sir saaee naa nivai so sir keejai kaane |
lines.english: That head which does not bow to the Lord - what is to be done with that head?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁੰਨੇ ਹੇਠਿ ਜਲਾਈਐ ਬਾਲਣ ਸੰਦੈ ਥਾਇ ॥੭੨॥
lines.translit: kune hetth jalaaeeai baalan sandai thaae |72|
lines.english: Put it in the fireplace, instead of firewood. ||72||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਿਥੈ ਤੈਡੇ ਮਾਪਿਆ ਜਿਨੑੀ ਤੂ ਜਣਿਓਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kithai taidde maapiaa jinaee too janiohi |
lines.english: Fareed, where are your mother and father, who gave birth to you?

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਪਾਸਹੁ ਓਇ ਲਦਿ ਗਏ ਤੂੰ ਅਜੈ ਨ ਪਤੀਣੋਹਿ ॥੭੩॥
lines.translit: tai paasahu oe lad ge toon ajai na pateenohi |73|
lines.english: They have left you, but even so, you are not convinced that you shall also have to go. ||73||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੈ ਮੂਲਿ ਨ ਆਵਸੀ ਦੋਜਕ ਸੰਦੀ ਭਾਹਿ ॥੭੪॥
lines.translit: agai mool na aavasee dojak sandee bhaeh |74|
lines.english: Hereafter, the fires of hell shall not even approach you. ||74||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਮਨੁ ਮੈਦਾਨੁ ਕਰਿ ਟੋਏ ਟਿਬੇ ਲਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa man maidaan kar ttoe ttibe laeh |
lines.english: Fareed, flatten out your mind; smooth out the hills and valleys.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਖਾਲਕੁ ਖਲਕ ਮਹਿ ਖਲਕ ਵਸੈ ਰਬ ਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa khaalak khalak meh khalak vasai rab maeh |
lines.english: Fareed, the Creator is in the Creation, and the Creation abides in God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਦਾ ਕਿਸ ਨੋ ਆਖੀਐ ਜਾਂ ਤਿਸੁ ਬਿਨੁ ਕੋਈ ਨਾਹਿ ॥੭੫॥
lines.translit: mandaa kis no aakheeai jaan tis bin koee naeh |75|
lines.english: Whom can we call bad? There is none without Him. ||75||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਿ ਦਿਹਿ ਨਾਲਾ ਕਪਿਆ ਜੇ ਗਲੁ ਕਪਹਿ ਚੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa ji dihi naalaa kapiaa je gal kapeh chukh |
lines.english: Fareed, if on that day when my umbilical cord was cut, my throat had been cut instead,

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਵਨਿ ਨ ਇਤੀ ਮਾਮਲੇ ਸਹਾਂ ਨ ਇਤੀ ਦੁਖ ॥੭੬॥
lines.translit: pavan na itee maamale sahaan na itee dukh |76|
lines.english: I would not have fallen into so many troubles, or undergone so many hardships. ||76||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਬਣ ਚਲਣ ਰਤੰਨ ਸੇ ਸੁਣੀਅਰ ਬਹਿ ਗਏ ॥
lines.translit: chaban chalan ratan se suneear beh ge |
lines.english: My teeth, feet, eyes and ears have stopped working.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੇੜੇ ਮੁਤੀ ਧਾਹ ਸੇ ਜਾਨੀ ਚਲਿ ਗਏ ॥੭੭॥
lines.translit: herre mutee dhaah se jaanee chal ge |77|
lines.english: My body cries out, "Those whom I knew have left me!" ||77||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਬੁਰੇ ਦਾ ਭਲਾ ਕਰਿ ਗੁਸਾ ਮਨਿ ਨ ਹਢਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa bure daa bhalaa kar gusaa man na hadtaae |
lines.english: Fareed, answer evil with goodness; do not fill your mind with anger.
